name: Dolmades Yialandzi (Stuffed Grapevine Leaves)
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat the olive oil in a skillet over medium heat.
2. Add the onion and scallions, sauté until soft and transparent, about 5 minutes.
3. Add garlic and cook for a few minutes, then add rice, dill, parsley, lemon juice, salt, pepper, and remaining olive oil.
4. Stir well to combine all ingredients.
5. Add hot water and simmer covered for about 5 minutes. Remove from heat and allow to cool.
6. Carefully remove grapevine leaves from the jar, leaving brine in the jar.
7. Wash leaves thoroughly, drain, then cut heavy stems off each leaf using a sharp knife.
8. Line an enameled pan with several heavy grapevine leaves.
9. To stuff: Place a leaf rough side up on working surface, stem end near you.
10. Add teaspoonful of rice mixture near stem end and fold the leaf portion near you over the filling.
11. Fold right side of leaf over filling, then left side, rolling tightly away from you towards pointed end.
12. Place dolma seam side down in prepared pan.
13. Repeat until all filling is used. Store any unused leaves in brine for later use.
14. Cover dolmades with an inverted plate and add enough water to cover (about 1-1/2 cups).
15. Bring to a boil, then lower heat and simmer slowly for 1-1/4 hours.
16. Taste rice; if not tender, continue cooking slowly until done.
17. Cool, then chill before serving cold as an appetizer or entree.
Note: In Macedonia and Thrace, add raisins and pine nuts to the filling when adding rice. Adjust dolma size by varying filling amount but keep portions consistent for equal cooking times. Store refrigerated up to a week.
